title: Unit 04 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Credit, Debt, and Loans.

choices.text: The ability to borrow money and repay it later
choices.feedback: Correct. Credit gives a person access to borrowed money that must be repaid later.

choices.text: Money that never has to be paid back
choices.feedback: Borrowed money through credit must be repaid.

choices.text: Only money saved in a bank account
choices.feedback: Credit is about borrowing, not simply storing saved money.

choices.text: A tax added to all purchases
choices.feedback: Credit is not a tax.

questions.prompt: Making payments on time is usually stronger for credit than missing payments.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: On-time payment behavior is usually stronger because it supports responsible borrowing.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. On-time payments are a stronger borrowing behavior than missed payments.

questions.prompt: Which type of loan is most closely connected to buying a home?

choices.text: Mortgage
choices.feedback: A mortgage is the correct loan type for a home purchase, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Credit card
choices.feedback: Credit cards are not the main borrowing tool for home purchases.

choices.text: Mortgage
choices.feedback: Correct. Mortgages are loans commonly used to buy homes or property.

choices.text: Student loan
choices.feedback: Student loans are connected to education expenses, not home purchases.

questions.prompt: A borrower takes a $300 loan with $24 in interest and a $6 fee. What is the total repayment amount?

choices.feedback: Add the borrowed amount, interest, and fee together carefully.
This is the correct total, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.
This total is too high for the numbers in the scenario.
Correct. $300 plus $24 plus $6 equals $324.

questions.prompt: Interest and fees can increase the total cost of borrowing.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Interest and fees raise the amount a borrower must repay.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Interest and fees do affect the total amount repaid.

questions.prompt: Which borrowing pattern is the stronger example of debt management?

choices.text: Skipping payments and borrowing again to cover older debt
choices.feedback: That pattern can create deeper debt problems.

choices.text: Making payments on time and following a repayment plan
choices.feedback: Correct. Responsible repayment is the stronger debt management pattern.

choices.text: Ignoring total repayment amounts
choices.feedback: Ignoring total repayment can lead to weaker borrowing decisions.

choices.text: Choosing debt without comparing consequences
choices.feedback: Strong borrowing decisions should compare consequences before borrowing.

questions.prompt: Why is comparing total repayment more useful than looking only at the amount borrowed?

choices.text: Because the total repayment shows the full borrowing cost
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because interest and fees never matter
choices.feedback: Interest and fees do matter because they affect the real cost.

choices.text: Because the total repayment shows the full borrowing cost
choices.feedback: Correct. The total repayment includes the original amount plus added borrowing costs.

choices.text: Because all loans cost the same in the end
choices.feedback: Loans can cost different amounts depending on interest and fees.

questions.prompt: Which is the strongest reason to avoid borrowing more than can realistically be repaid?

choices.text: Because debt can grow into a long-term financial problem
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.

choices.text: Because repayment ability does not matter
choices.feedback: Repayment ability is one of the most important parts of responsible borrowing.

choices.text: Because all forms of borrowing are automatically harmful
choices.feedback: Borrowing is not always harmful, but it must be used responsibly.

choices.text: Because debt can grow into a long-term financial problem
choices.feedback: Correct. Borrowing more than can be repaid can create serious long-term financial risk.
